the bug**

upon opening Nora I am greeted with `Load function 'load' not found.` and the message `window.bundleEntries.chartUtilities is undefined` in console.

**Galaxy Version and/or server at which you observed the bug**
Galaxy Version:  24.1.4.dev0 
Commit: it's usegalaxy.org today

**Browser and Operating System**
Operating System: linux
Browser: Firefox

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:
1. Go to https://usegalaxy.org/u/helena-rasche/h/unnamed-history-2
2. import
3. open nora
4. it doesn't show a dataset selector, nor does it show nora's interface (unlike https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/11468) it just immediately errors

visualizations","user":{"login":"davelopez","id":46503462,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ2NTAzNDYy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/46503462?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez","html_url":"https://github.com/davelopez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"There are some visualization plugins that can work directly with remote URIs (see #19061). This PR adds a new attribute `allow_uri_if_protocol` to test conditions for visualizations that tells the visualization registry that a visualization plugin can work with a deferred dataset of a particular datatype and allows some URI schemes. By default, this attribute is set to an empty list, so it is opt-in. An empty list means there is no support for deferred datasets. You can also use the wildcard "*" to indicate that any protocol is supported.

For example:
```xml
<test type="isinstance" test_attr="datatype" result_type="datatype" allow_uri_if_protocol="https,s3">data.ZarrDirectory</test>
```

## How to test the changes?
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  - Upload a deferred dataset
  - Install a galaxy visualization that can work with remote URIs (an example is here https://github.com/davelopez/galaxy-visualizations/tree/vizarr/packages/vizarr or check out #19061)
  - Observe that deferred datasets will show associated visualizations if they pass the test.

## License

{"action":"closed","number":19097,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/19097","id":2157814259,"node_id":"PR_kwDOAdw-Nc6AnaHz","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19097","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19097.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19097.patch","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/19097","number":19097,"state":"closed","locked":false,"title":"Support deferred datasets in visualizations","user":{"login":"davelopez","id":46503462,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjQ2NTAzNDYy","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/46503462?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez","html_url":"https://github.com/davelopez","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/davelopez/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"body":"There are some visualization plugins that can work directly with remote URIs (see #19061). This PR adds a new attribute `allow_uri_if_protocol` to test conditions for visualizations that tells the visualization registry that a visualization plugin can work with a deferred dataset of a particular datatype and allows some URI schemes. By default, this attribute is set to an empty list, so it is opt-in. An empty list means there is no support for deferred datasets. You can also use the wildcard "*" to indicate that any protocol is supported.

For example:
```xml
<test type="isinstance" test_attr="datatype" result_type="datatype" allow_uri_if_protocol="https,s3">data.ZarrDirectory</test>
```

## How to test the changes?
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  - Upload a deferred dataset
  - Install a galaxy visualization that can work with remote URIs (an example is here https://github.com/davelopez/galaxy-visualizations/tree/vizarr/packages/vizarr or check out #19061)
  - Observe that deferred datasets will show associated visualizations if they pass the test.

## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

linking upload parameters will still be respected, but if none of them are set data fetch will default to just linking files during upload. This uses Dataset.external_filename instead of symlinks in the objectstore so that Galaxy has better tracking of the links and so this works closer to the way data libraries have always worked.

Alternative to https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19125.

ml_url":"https://github.com/jdavcs","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"open_issues":62,"closed_issues":237,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-11-13T13:59:55Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-10-16T09:48:55Z","updated_at":"2024-11-13T14:22:19Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/19003","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19003","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19003.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/19003.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"This plugin goal is to enable astrophysicists to explore and plot FITS file table and binary table data directly inside Galaxy. To achieve this goal the plugin main package astrovisjs https://github.com/esg-epfl-apc/astrojsvis relies on : - fits-reader javascript library https://github.com/esg-epfl-apc/fits-reader - BokehJS graph library https://docs.bokeh.org/en/latest/docs/user_guide/advanced/bokehjs.html - D3 graph library https://d3js.org/ All packages are loaded with npm and the plugin is built using webpack Plugin main features are : - Explore any FITS file headers - Explore table and bintable data in table format - Load local files when dataset relies on external files data - Multiple files handling - Automatic column creation based on header data - Dynamically plot data using D3 or BokehJS from user choices (columns, ranges, error bars, log, .....) - Error bars support - Arithmetic columns creation ## How to test the changes? - [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows: 1. Load a FITS file containing at least one table or binary table HDU, example file available here https://www.astro.unige.ch/mmoda/gallery/data-products/1e-11451-6141-isgri-light-curve-61 at the very bottom of the page 2. Launch plugin with selected file 3. Select file from the "available files" column and use the "Add to plot" button at the bottom to switch it to "current files" column 4. Select value for X Y axis and error bars if needed (for instance TIME, RATE, ERROR, TIMEDEL) 5. #19040 and #19097
xref #16862

Visualization for Zarr-like datasets based on https://github.com/hms-dbmi/vizarr

## For local Zarr datasets (directories uploaded as zip)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75806c9e-6ce0-4eb5-8b9d-ea32cbbb1a45

## For remote (deferred) Zarr datasets (URI pointing to an S3 or remote location)


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f342eff-2f18-4951-86b6-e60e460a273c




## TODO
- [x] Move the visualization to the new "pre-built visualizations" best practice
- [x] Fix ZarrDirectory datatype not being associated with the visualization ([all the other datatypes work](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/compare/dev...davelopez:add_vizarr_visualization?expand=1#diff-93c6e01827e0977bf358dc19da62dc8def73af7112de78ca9aa4ed769fd7b428R9-R12)...)

## How to test the changes?
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  - Using local Zarr datasets
    - Upload a zarr.zip or ome_zarr.zip file with image data
    - Unpack the zip to a directory using the appropriate datatype converter
    - Check the Vizarr visualization 
  - Using remote Zarr datasets (public)
    - Go to a public Zarr repository, for example, https://www.openmicroscopy.org/2020/11/04/zarr-data.html
    - Copy any link to a zarr-based store and paste it into the Galaxy upload dialog
    - Make sure you set `defer dataset resolution` in the upload options before uploading
    - Once it is uploaded, check the Vizarr visualization for the deferred dataset


## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

xref #16862

Visualization for Zarr-like datasets based on https://github.com/hms-dbmi/vizarr

## For local Zarr datasets (directories uploaded as zip)

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/75806c9e-6ce0-4eb5-8b9d-ea32cbbb1a45

## For remote (deferred) Zarr datasets (URI pointing to an S3 or remote location)


https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4f342eff-2f18-4951-86b6-e60e460a273c




## TODO
- [x] Move the visualization to the new "pre-built visualizations" best practice
- [x] Fix ZarrDirectory datatype not being associated with the visualization ([all the other datatypes work](https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/compare/dev...davelopez:add_vizarr_visualization?expand=1#diff-93c6e01827e0977bf358dc19da62dc8def73af7112de78ca9aa4ed769fd7b428R9-R12)...)

## How to test the changes?
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  - Using local Zarr datasets
    - Upload a zarr.zip or ome_zarr.zip file with image data
    - Unpack the zip to a directory using the appropriate datatype converter
    - Check the Vizarr visualization 
  - Using remote Zarr datasets (public)
    - Go to a public Zarr repository, for example, https://www.openmicroscopy.org/2020/11/04/zarr-data.html
    - Copy any link to a zarr-based store and paste it into the Galaxy upload dialog
    - Make sure you set `defer dataset resolution` in the upload options before uploading
    - Once it is uploaded, check the Vizarr visualization for the deferred dataset


## License
- [x] I agree to license these and all my past contributions to the core galaxy codebase under the [MIT license](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT).

There is one issue with this PR. We recently moved away from committing visualization specific scripts directly into the repo. The Galaxy Visualization XMLs allow installing npm dependencies using the `<requirements>` tag.

`<requirements>
        <requirement type="npm" version="0.0.1" package="your_npm_package"/>
</requirements>`

Just make sure that the `npm run build` command produces a `static` or `dist/static` directory with your js-script files. By moving the js-script files out of the Galaxy repo, we ensure proper versioning of visualization plugins and keep the core repo size at bay. Examples for this approach are the `msa` and `ngl` viewers.

Please let me know if you have any questions or need further Added a visualizer for molecular structure files, using jsmol library.
Source of the contents in the static directory is from the original source of [Jmol](https://sourceforge.net/projects/jmol/), which is part of the pull request but included from the source as it it.
Screenshot of the visualizer:
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b8c561e4-cf41-4cd3-a779-bdd91cbc84d7)


## How to test the changes?
(Select all options that apply)
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [ ] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [x] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. upload the follwing files and visualizeit [cif file](http://www.crystallography.net/cod/1000001.cif), [pdb file](https://files.rcsb.org/view/6r3q.pdb)

#18848

When we have multiple data inputs in a tool, the ToolForm will request a tool rebuild for each data input in rapid succession (see https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18848#issuecomment-2399667355).

This will ensure that only the last request with all the updated data is sent.

## Before

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da15efb1-7ee2-44e2-aa75-1b37ba4c9a1b)

## After

![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9233244a-a1e4-4493-9618-7df3398b9772)



## How to test the changes?
- [ ] I've included appropriate [automated tests](https://docs.galaxyproject.org/en/latest/dev/writing_tests.html).
- [x] This is a refactoring of components with existing test coverage.
- [ ] Instructions for manual testing are as follows:
  1. [add testing steps and prerequisites here if you didn't write automated tests covering all your changes]

ub.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":31,"closed_issues":6,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-06-13T16:40:37Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-04-07T08:56:58Z","updated_at":"2024-06-28T10:58:27Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/17927","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17927","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17927.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17927.patch","merged_at":null},"body":"The goal of this PR is to achieve consistency and reduce redundancy in the Masthead and align its functionality with the Activity Bar. The PR also contains improvements to the Activity Bar and Masthead as recently discussed during the UX team meeting.

1) @mvdbeek suggested that hidden and less frequently used activities should still be readily accessible. To account for that, the `Settings` activity has been replaced with a `More` activity. Users may still use this activity to add or remove items from the activity bar. Additionally, now users may also directly navigate to any activity using this panel.

2) @davelopez suggested that the username should be visible to the user and that accessing user preferences should remain in the masthead dropdown. This idea has been implemented, the username has been added to the masthead, user preferences and logout are accessible through the user tab. 

Resolves #17476, #17109, #17513.

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/2105447/a8b16076-792b-4c05-b864-c9156b7d3ac8

goal of this PR is to achieve consistency and reduce redundancy in the Masthead and align its functionality with the Activity Bar. The PR also contains improvements to the Activity Bar and Masthead as recently discussed during the UX team meeting.

1) @mvdbeek suggested that hidden and less frequently used activities should still be readily accessible. To account for that, the `Settings` activity has been replaced with a `More` activity. Users may still use this activity to add or remove items from the activity bar. Additionally, now users may also directly navigate to any activity using this panel.

2) @davelopez suggested that the username should be visible to the user and that accessing user preferences should remain in the masthead dropdown. This idea has been implemented, the username has been added to the masthead, user preferences and logout are accessible through the user tab. 

Resolves #17476, #17109, #17513.

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/2105447/a8b16076-792b-4c05-b864-c9156b7d3ac8

The PR also contains improvements to the Activity Bar and Masthead as recently discussed during the UX team meeting.

1) @mvdbeek suggested that hidden and less frequently used activities should still be readily accessible. To account for that, the `Settings` activity has been replaced with a `More` activity. Users may still use this activity to add or remove items from the activity bar. Additionally, now users may also directly navigate to any activity using this panel.

2) @davelopez suggested that the username should be visible to the user and that accessing user preferences should remain in the masthead dropdown. This idea has been implemented, the username has been added to the masthead, user preferences and logout are accessible through the user tab. 

Resolves #17476, #17109, #17513.

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/2105447/a8b16076-792b-4c05-b864-c9156b7d3ac8

thub.com/users/jdavcs/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/jdavcs/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"open_issues":32,"closed_issues":7,"state":"open","created_at":"2024-05-14T14:03:11Z","updated_at":"2024-06-29T08:17:26Z","due_on":null,"closed_at":null},"comments":6,"created_at":"2024-04-07T08:56:58Z","updated_at":"2024-06-29T08:18:08Z","closed_at":"2024-06-29T08:17:25Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"draft":false,"pull_request":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/galaxyproject/galaxy/pulls/17927","html_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17927","diff_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17927.diff","patch_url":"https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/pull/17927.patch","merged_at":"2024-06-29T08:17:25Z"},"body":"The goal of this PR is to achieve consistency and reduce redundancy in the Masthead and align its functionality with the Activity Bar. The PR also contains improvements to the Activity Bar and Masthead as recently discussed during the UX team meeting.

1) @mvdbeek suggested that hidden and less frequently used activities should still be readily accessible. To account for that, the `Settings` activity has been replaced with a `More` activity. Users may still use this activity to add or remove items from the activity bar. Additionally, now users may also directly navigate to any activity using this panel.

2) @davelopez suggested that the username should be visible to the user and that accessing user preferences should remain in the masthead dropdown. This idea has been implemented, the username has been added to the masthead, user preferences and logout are accessible through the user tab. 

Resolves #17476, #17109, #17513.

https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/assets/2105447/a8b16076-792b-4c05-b864-c9156b7d3ac8

issue: https://github.com/galaxyproject/galaxy/issues/18444

The name was changed from `plausible.js` to `script.js` in the snippet or something like that. When adding a host to the `plausible.galaxyproject.eu` the following snippet `<script defer data-domain="usegalaxy.eu" src="https://plausible.galaxyproject.eu/js/script.js"></script>
` is created that should be added to the `<Head>` of a website. Locally replaced this with `script.js` and the stats are getting collected. PR consolidates data dialog components and converts them to composition api and type script. This is a preparation for upcoming changes to the data dialog functionality. Additionally this PR decomposes some of the nested abstraction layers. Moving forward it should be easier for us to make additions or customize the data dialog for use in different contexts.
